mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50,000 persons in single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in real retail sales and food services signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the earnings revision cycle turns negative.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 700,727 million dollars as of 2024-02-15
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS shows 3-month decline > 1.5%, or Spearman rank correlation < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increase in industrial production index signals expanding productive capacity and improving corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as supply-side strength supports earnings growth.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months when INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts and retail sales signals synchronized contraction across residential and consumer demand channels, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices cyclical growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% AND RSAFS declines > 1.0% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,331 thousand units as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST decline × RSAFS decline) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate combined with declining payroll employment signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings downgrades cascade through the market.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines > 100,000 in same period
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal (UNRATE rise AND PAYEMS decline) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI remains elevated above 3.0% while the federal funds rate is held above 5.0%, signaling the Fed maintains restrictive policy stance that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or persistent inflation regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (high inflation × high rates) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly remains below 2.0% annualized rate while housing starts decline, signaling demand-side weakness that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations and cyclical earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.0% in latest quarter AND HOUST declines > 3% in same quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,668.986 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal (low GDP growth AND housing decline) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
